entries.definition:
d–s–y to corrupt, to soil, to let down, to tempt. Of this root,
دَسَّى dassā occurs once in the Qur’an.

دَسَّى dassā [v. II, trans.] to corrupt, to tarnish (91:7–10) قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن
زَكَّاهَا وَقَدْ خَابَ مَن دَسَّاهَا the one who purifies it [his soul] succeeds
and the one who corrupts it fails.
